query: Set up my job-search profile before we start looking for jobs
rationale: Direct request to set up the North-Star profile - the canonical profile-intake trigger. Maps to init_profile.py + the 5 questions.

query: Let me tell you about myself and what kind of role I actually want before we search
rationale: User wants to capture who they are beyond the resume (target archetype, preferences). This is the North-Star intake, not a search yet.

query: Here's my resume DOCX - can you read it and pull out my experience?
rationale: Resume parsing/extraction request. Exercises parse_resume.py (DOCX path). Profile-intake reads the resume; it does not tailor it.

query: Extract the text from my resume PDF so we can use it
rationale: Plain resume-to-text extraction. Direct parse_resume.py PDF path; downstream members consume the parsed text.

query: I set up my profile a few months ago - can we refresh it?
rationale: Stale-profile refresh. Exercises init_profile.py read + the 90-day staleness prompt; does not re-walk all five questions.

query: Capture my deal-breakers: no on-call, no defense work, no relocation
rationale: User is supplying North-Star answers (deal-breakers question). Profile-intake records these into .job-hunter-profile.md.

query: Tailor my resume for this product manager role at Stripe
rationale: Sibling-collision negative. Tailoring a resume to a specific posting is resume-tailor's job, not profile-intake. career-profile parses resumes but never tailors them.

query: Find me senior backend engineer roles in Seattle, $180k+
rationale: Sibling-collision negative. Finding/scoring postings is job-search (or the orchestrator). Profile-intake does not search.

query: Review my resume and tell me what's wrong with it
rationale: Generic resume critique divorced from a job search. Out of scope for profile-intake, which reads/parses but does not critique.

query: Should I become a product manager?
rationale: Career-coaching question with no concrete job search or profile setup. Out of scope across the family.
